# datalock

> Privacy-by-Design for tabular data in Python.
> LGPD/GDPR compliance, PII masking, AES-256-GCM encryption (.dlk),
> expressive DSL over Polars, canary data, semantic text masking.

Renamed from logus-lgpd. `import logus as lg` still works.

## Installation

```bash
pip install datalock
pip install "datalock[sql]"         # DuckDB
pip install "datalock[synthetic]"   # Faker for richer synthetic data
pip install "datalock[full]"
```

## Always import as

```python
import datalock as dd
import os

SALT = os.environ["DATALOCK_SALT"]
KEY  = os.environ["DATALOCK_KEY"]
# SALT and KEY must be different values
```

## Core patterns

```python
df       = dd.read("file.csv")                    # any format → pl.DataFrame
df       = dd.read("file.dlk", key=KEY)           # decrypt .dlk
df       = dd.read("big.parquet", head=100_000)   # partial read, no OOM
reports  = dd.scan(df)                            # detect PII columns
df_safe  = dd.mask(df, salt=SALT)                 # mask PII, preserves type
dd.store(df_safe, "out.dlk", key=KEY)             # encrypt and save
result   = dd.process("in.csv", salt=SALT, key=KEY, output="out.dlk")
```

## New in v1.0.1 (datalock)

### Canary data — transparent leak tracing
```python
# Canary rows injected silently, stripped on read. User never sees them.
dd.store(df, "f.dlk", key=KEY, canary=True)
df_back = dd.read("f.dlk", key=KEY)         # df_back.shape == df.shape

# When breach token found in dump:
dd.canary_check("canary.1ba472d8@datalock.internal")
# → {"pipeline_id": "crm_jan2025", "filepath": "f.dlk", "injected_at": ...}
```

### Semantic text masking
```python
text = "CPF 111.444.777-35, email joao@empresa.com"

dd.mask_text(text, salt=SALT, strategy="redact")   # → "[CPF], [EMAIL]"
dd.mask_text(text, salt=SALT, strategy="hash")     # → "3f2a8b1c, 9e1d3c7f"
dd.mask_text(text, salt=SALT, strategy="semantic") # → "478.622.984-97, roberto.santos@gmail.com"
# semantic: valid fake CPF (checksum correct), no faker required
# deterministic: same input + same salt → same fake value (preserves joins)
```

### Directory PII inventory
```python
inventory = dd.scan_directory("./dados/", recursive=True, min_risk="medium")
print(inventory.summary())
inventory.to_html("inventory.html")
for path, fi in inventory.items():
    print(f"{path}: {fi.max_risk} — {list(fi.pii_columns.keys())}")
```

### Audit webhook
```python
dd.configure(audit_webhook="https://hooks.slack.com/T.../B.../xxx")
# Every dd.mask(), dd.store(), dd.scan() fires a POST (non-blocking, best-effort)
```

## Key functions

### I/O
| Function | Notes |
|---|---|
| `dd.read(path)` | Any format → pl.DataFrame |
| `dd.read(path, head=N)` | First N rows |
| `dd.read(path, sample=N)` | Parquet: random row groups; CSV: head(N) with warning |
| `dd.read(path, n_chunks=5, chunks=[2,4])` | Partial read by chunk |
| `dd.read(path, iter_chunks=True)` | Generator, no OOM |
| `dd.read(path, header_only=True)` | Schema + shape, zero data read |
| `dd.read(banco, "table")` | From DatabaseConnection |
| `dd.store(df, "out.dlk", key=KEY)` | AES-256-GCM encrypted |
| `dd.store(df, ..., canary=True)` | With transparent canary rows |
| `dd.store(df, ..., expires_at="2025-12-31")` | Expiry enforced on read |
| `dd.store(df, ..., public_key=pub)` | Asymmetric encryption |
| `dd.read(f, private_key=priv)` | Decrypt asymmetric |

### Privacy / LGPD
| Function | Notes |
|---|---|
| `dd.scan(df)` | → Dict[str, ColumnReport] |
| `dd.scan(df, custom_patterns={...})` | + company-specific identifiers |
| `dd.mask(df, salt=SALT)` | HMAC-SHA256, deterministic |
| `dd.mask(lf, salt=SALT)` | LazyFrame stays lazy |
| `dd.mask_text(text, strategy=)` | "redact"/"hash"/"partial"/"semantic" |
| `dd.scan_directory(path)` | Directory PII inventory |
| `dd.compliance_report(df, reports)` | HTML/PDF LGPD report |
| `dd.validate_schema(df, ...)` | Schema-level validation |
| `dd.canary_check(token)` | Look up breach token in manifest |

### Manipulation (pd→pd, pl→pl, lazy→lazy)
```python
dd.where(df, uf="SP")
dd.where(df, uf=["SP","RJ"])
dd.where(df, renda=(5_000, 15_000))
dd.sort(df, "renda", desc=True)
dd.groupby(df, "uf", {"n":("*","count"), "media":("renda","mean")})
dd.add_column(df, taxa=dd.col("renda")*0.27)
dd.when(dd.col("renda")>10_000,"alta").otherwise("baixa")
dd.shift(df, 1)          # lag
dd.lead(df, 1)           # lead
dd.explode(df, "tags")   # list column → rows
dd.pivot(df, index="uf", columns="tipo", values="renda")
```

### Database
```python
banco = dd.db("postgresql://user:pass@host/db", salt=SALT)
df    = dd.read(banco, "clientes")
df    = dd.read(banco, "SELECT * FROM c WHERE uf='SP'")
df    = dd.read(banco, "clientes", sample=10_000)
banco.create_table(df, "clientes", if_exists="replace")
banco.upsert(df_new, "clientes", on="cpf")
banco.write(df_safe, "clientes_masked")
banco.tables(); banco.schema("clientes")
with dd.db("sqlite:///db.sqlite") as banco: ...
```

### Synthetic data
```python
gen = dd.SyntheticGenerator(seed=42)
gen.cpf()   # "478.622.984-97" — valid checksum, no faker needed
gen.cnpj()  # "91.202.089/8546-89"
gen.email() # "roberto.santos@gmail.com"
gen.nome()  # "Maria Aparecida"
gen.generate("cpf")  # same as gen.cpf()
# If faker installed (pip install datalock[synthetic]): uses Faker pt_BR automatically
```

## Critical rules for AI code generation

1. ALWAYS `import datalock as dd` — never `from datalock import *`
2. NEVER hardcode SALT or KEY — always `os.environ["DATALOCK_SALT"]`
3. SALT and KEY must be DIFFERENT values
4. NEVER `open("file.dlk")` — always `dd.read("file.dlk", key=KEY)`
5. `dd.when(...).when(...).otherwise(default)` — always end with `.otherwise()`
6. `dd.mask()` without `salt=` generates random salt — JOINs will break
7. `dd.col` IS `polars.col` — all 200+ Polars methods work
8. All manipulation functions preserve input type (pd→pd, pl→pl, lazy→lazy)

## .dlk format

AES-256-GCM + HKDF-SHA256 + Parquet/zstd. Magic: `b"DLOCK"` (v1.0.1+).
Backward compat: `b"LOGUS"` (.lgs files from logus-lgpd) still readable.

## Backward compat

```python
import logus as lg   # still works — alias to datalock
lg.mask(df, salt=SALT)  # identical to dd.mask()
dd.read("old_file.lgs", key=KEY)  # .lgs files readable
```
